Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me analyze the log to understand the behavior First let me look at the traces Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC Let me identify the pattern Always starts with RA Then VF Then AE Then optionally a loop involving RMI and VF

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the typical pattern for do A then optionally repeat B then A is loop But here we have AE optional loop AD What happens in the loop Case 1 AE RMI VF AD Case 2 AE AD Case 3 AE RMI RMI VF AD

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: If the loop is VF RMI that would mean execute VF then either exit or execute RMI then VF again But we dont see VF immediately after AE in Cases 1 and 3 we see RMI first

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the LOOP semantics A B execute A then either exit or execute B then A again If A RMI B tau Execute RMI Choice exit Trace RMI or execute tau then RMI again Trace RMI RMI etc So one or more RMI

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: the structure after AE X tau -> * RMI tau VF Then AD

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Traces tau nothing RMI VF RMI RMI VF Matches the log perfectly Case 2 skip Case 1 RMI VF Case 3 RMI RMI VF

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Alternative tau RMI Execute tau either exit empty or RMI then tau again VF VF RMI VF But allows VF without RMI AE VF AD which is NOT in the log VF only appears after RMI Reject

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the model allows RA VF AE AD SC AC RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC No disallowed traces Does not allow RMI without VF VF without RMI etc Perfect fit

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: the loop with tau is elegant balances fitness precision and simplicity right choice over more complex XOR nesting
